feedback.text: I believe games should be available in physical form and remain fully playable regardless of server status or expiration policies. When a game depends on constant internet access, or bound to servers, its lifespan becomes artificially limited. This not only undermines consumer rights, but also damages long term preservation of gaming as a cultural medium. Physical copies give players true ownership and ensure that the games they purchase remain accessible, even decades later. We need industry practices that respect both player investment and historical preservation by allowing games to function offline and beyond their original lifecycle. Regards
